name: Maple Rice Pudding
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the milk and rice in a large saucepan.
2. Cook, stirring over medium-low heat until the mixture boils and thickens, about 25 minutes.
3. Stir in 1/3 cup maple syrup and cook for an additional 10 minutes.
4. Add the grated orange rind and stir gently.
5. Pour the pudding into four (8-ounce) dessert bowls or custard cups.
6. Heat the walnuts in a small heavy frying pan over low heat, stirring until fragrant, about 3 minutes.
7. Drizzle with the remaining 2 tablespoons maple syrup.
8. Cook the walnuts, stirring, until the syrup boils and coats them, about 2 minutes.
9. Sprinkle the cooked walnuts on top of each pudding.
Per Serving: 377 calories, 6.9 g fat (16% of calories), 1.5 g dietary fiber, 12.5 g protein, 66.3 g carbohydrates, 4 mg cholesterol, 135 mg sodium.
Source: Prevention, January 1993
